fix_snippets.apache:
# Apache — add to .htaccess or <VirtualHost>
Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none';"
Header always set Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin"
Header always set Permissions-Policy "camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), interest-cohort=()"
Header always set Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy "same-origin"
Header always set Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy "same-origin"

weaknesses.description: No Content-Security-Policy header — all inline scripts execute freely

File upload via PUT — may allow arbitrary file writes
Resource deletion — may allow unauthorized deletions
PATCH method accepted — verify authorization controls

recommendations: DNSSEC not enabled — DNS responses can be spoofed

block_patterns: Custom error page with Akamai reference
block_behavior.timing_signature: blocked responses slightly faster due to edge evaluation

bypass_strategies.description: Akamai edge and backend may disagree on request boundaries
bypass_strategies.payload_example: CL.TE desync between Akamai edge and origin

bypass_strategies.description: Some Akamai configs only inspect GET/POST, method override bypasses
HPP — Akamai may only inspect first or last param value

config_gaps: SQLi expected to be blocked but was not — possible config gap

technique_matrix.notes: Most common bypass — works against vendors that don't recursively URL-decode
Fullwidth Unicode, overlong UTF-8, or homoglyph substitution
Send payload in unexpected Content-Type (text/plain, text/xml, multipart)
Payload in JSON body when WAF only inspects URI/headers
AWS WAF default body inspection limit is 8KB — pad before payload
HTTP Parameter Pollution — duplicate params with different values
Only works if origin IP is exposed — use DNS history, crt.sh, Shodan
BISO-proxied zones may not apply WAF on HTTP/1.1 vs HTTP/2
CL.TE or TE.CL desync between CDN edge and origin
Split attack across multiple rules, each below anomaly score threshold

gap_analysis.risk_summary: HIGH — 3 viable bypass techniques, 0 signature gaps, 0 anomaly gaps, 1 config discrepancies

findings.finding: Staging/dev environment(s): apply-device-check.card.rakuten.co.jp, dev-chat-support.mobile.rakuten.co.jp, dev.business.ai.rakuten.co.jp, old-test-ftp.bi.admintool.rakuten.co.jp, preview.rms.rakuten.co.jp
DNS hygiene score 75/100 (grade B) — 2 check(s) failed
DNSSEC not enabled — DNS responses can be spoofed
WAF (Akamai (Kona/App & API Protector)) detected on redirect target (rakuten.co.jp -> www.rakuten.co.jp), not the original domain

attack_vectors.description: Subdomains with no CDN or WAF protection, directly exposed on the internet.
attack_vectors.impact: No edge security — vulnerable to direct attacks, DDoS, and automated scanning.
attack_vectors.detail: 114 subdomain(s) have no WAF or CDN protection. Examples: ac.rakuten.co.jp, ad-api.reward.rakuten.co.jp, adsales.rakuten.co.jp … and 111 more. All traffic reaches origin servers directly — no DDoS scrubbing, bot filtering, or WAF inspection.

attack_vectors.description: Authentication and identity endpoints discovered — login portals, SSO, OAuth flows, password reset, and session management surfaces.
attack_vectors.impact: Credential stuffing, brute-force, session hijacking, and OAuth abuse can lead to full account compromise.
attack_vectors.mitre: T1078 — Valid Accounts / T1110 — Brute Force
attack_vectors.detail: Login/auth surface on rakuten.co.jp. Auth-related subdomains: blue-glogin.gl.rakuten.co.jp, cdc-mtwidget01.affiliate.rakuten.co.jp, glogin.rms.rakuten.co.jp, mtwidget04.affiliate.rakuten.co.jp, myid.rakuten.co.jp … and 4 more. Test for credential stuffing, brute-force (no rate limiting detected), session fixation, and OAuth misconfiguration.

attack_vectors.description: API endpoints discovered — REST, GraphQL, or internal service APIs that may lack proper authentication, rate limiting, or input validation.
attack_vectors.impact: Broken authentication, excessive data exposure, mass assignment, and SSRF via API abuse.
attack_vectors.detail: API attack surface: 23 API subdomain(s): ad-api.reward.rakuten.co.jp, api-ichiba-gateway.rakuten.co.jp, api.books.step.rakuten.co.jp, api.cms.rakuten.co.jp, api.map.rakuten.co.jp … and 18 more. attack_vectors.description: AI/ML and chatbot endpoints discovered — LLM-powered services that may be vulnerable to prompt injection, jailbreaking, and data exfiltration.
attack_vectors.impact: Prompt injection can bypass safety filters, leak system prompts, exfiltrate training data, or cause unintended actions.
attack_vectors.detail: 16 AI/chatbot subdomain(s): ai.rakuten.co.jp, assistant.ai.rakuten.co.jp, business.ai.rakuten.co.jp, chat.mobile.rakuten.co.jp, cleanedimage.ai.rakuten.co.jp … +11 more. 1 AI API path(s) discovered via active probing. Test for prompt injection, jailbreaking, system prompt leakage, and indirect prompt injection via user-supplied content.

attack_vectors.description: Payment processing, e-commerce, and financial transaction endpoints detected.
attack_vectors.impact: Price manipulation, payment bypass, card testing, and financial fraud.
attack_vectors.detail: 7 payment/commerce subdomain(s): checkout.rakuten.co.jp, front-listener.api.payment.rakuten.co.jp, listener.payment.rakuten.co.jp, pay.rakuten.co.jp, payment.rakuten.co.jp … and 2 more. Test for price manipulation, payment flow bypass, card testing, and PII exposure.

attack_vectors.description: Non-production environments publicly accessible — often with debug mode, verbose errors, default credentials, and weaker WAF rules.
attack_vectors.impact: Information disclosure, default credential access, code/config leakage, and pivot to production.
attack_vectors.detail: 6 staging/dev environment(s) publicly accessible: apply-device-check.card.rakuten.co.jp, dev-chat-support.mobile.rakuten.co.jp, dev.business.ai.rakuten.co.jp … and 3 more. Staging/dev environments typically have weaker WAF rules, debug mode enabled, verbose errors, and default credentials.

attack_vectors.description: Large number of unprotected subdomains without rate limiting — application-layer flood attacks possible.
attack_vectors.impact: Service degradation or outage via slow HTTP, resource-intensive queries, or connection exhaustion.
attack_vectors.detail: DDoS/L7 DoS surface: 114 unprotected subdomain(s) (no CDN): ac.rakuten.co.jp, ad-api.reward.rakuten.co.jp, adsales.rakuten.co.jp … and 111 more; 174 subdomains — large surface for distributed attacks. No rate limiting or scrubbing detected.

attack_vectors.description: Critical application endpoints discovered — login, registration, payment, admin, and API surfaces that are primary attack targets.
attack_vectors.impact: Credential theft, unauthorized access, payment fraud, privilege escalation, and data exfiltration.
attack_vectors.detail: 50 critical endpoint(s) discovered: 6 OAuth / OpenID, 5 MFA / 2FA, 9 Login / Authentication, 5 Password Reset, 8 User Registration, 6 API Auth Endpoint, 3 Session Management, 3 SSO / SAML, 5 API Endpoint. These endpoints handle sensitive operations and require hardened access controls, input validation, and monitoring.

attack_vectors.description: API keys, tokens, or credentials exposed in HTTP responses.
attack_vectors.impact: Account takeover, unauthorized API access, cloud resource compromise, and lateral movement.

attack_vectors.description: Public cloud storage buckets discovered (S3, Azure Blob, or GCS) with read or list access.
attack_vectors.impact: Data exfiltration, sensitive file exposure, backup leakage, and potential write access.

attack_vectors.description: JavaScript source maps publicly accessible — original source code, variable names, and internal paths can be recovered.
attack_vectors.impact: Source code disclosure, internal path discovery, hardcoded secret exposure, and business logic reverse engineering.

attack_vectors.description: Enterprise VPN concentrator detected — network perimeter entry point. VPN appliances are consistently targeted by ransomware groups and APTs via pre-auth RCE exploits.
attack_vectors.impact: Full internal network access via VPN exploitation, credential theft, lateral movement, and ransomware deployment.

attack_vectors.description: API documentation, specs, or gateway endpoints discovered on subdomains.
attack_vectors.impact: Subdomain APIs may have weaker access controls — full endpoint enumeration, auth bypass, and data exposure.
attack_vectors.detail: API security findings on 10 subdomain(s): api.recipe.rakuten.co.jp: 2 spec(s); api.cms.rakuten.co.jp: gateway: Envoy / Istio; api.map.rakuten.co.jp: gateway: API Gateway (generic); api.readee.rakuten.co.jp: gateway: AWS API Gateway; ad-api.reward.rakuten.co.jp: 1 spec(s)

attack_vectors.description: Public cloud storage buckets discovered via subdomain content analysis.
attack_vectors.impact: Data exfiltration, sensitive file exposure, and backup leakage via subdomain-referenced buckets.
attack_vectors.detail: 15 public bucket(s) found via subdomain scanning: dev-static (aws_s3) via dev.business.ai.rakuten.co.jp, dev-static (aws_s3) via dev.business.ai.rakuten.co.jp, dev-assets (gcs) via dev.business.ai.rakuten.co.jp, dev-assets (gcs) via dev.business.ai.rakuten.co.jp, cdn-assets (aws_s3) via cdn.rmc.contents.rakuten.co.jp

remediation.why: Prevents XSS, data injection, and clickjacking attacks
remediation.how: Add CSP header to web server config; start with report-only mode
Add missing headers to reverse proxy / web server configuration

remediation.why: No rate limiting — vulnerable to brute-force & L7 DDoS
remediation.how: Enable rate limiting on WAF/reverse proxy (e.g., 100 req/min per IP)

remediation.why: Staging environments often have weaker security controls
remediation.how: Add authentication, IP whitelist, or remove from public DNS

origin_ips.skip_reason: no CDN/WAF detected — target already resolves to origin
